## SINAMICS Parameter ACTIVE INFEED CONTROLMV 03663 - 06431

SINAMICS Parameter ACTIVE INFEED CONTROLMV 03663 - 06431

### p3663 VSM measuring voltage

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9880 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTORSL, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**All groups | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2001 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 1.00 [Vrms] | 690.00 [Vrms] | [ 0 ] 100.00 [Vrms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the supply voltage on the Voltage Sensing Module (VSM).

**Note:**
  
X522.1: Connection of L1  
X522.2: Connection of L2  
X522.3: Connection of L3  
The synchronizing voltage for a medium-voltage drive is stepped-down using an instrument
transformer. Generally, this does not mean that it has to have a secondary voltage
of 100 V (phase-phase, rms).

### p3900 Completion of quick commissioning

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Exits quick commissioning (p0010 = 1) with automatic calculation of all parameters
of all existing drive data sets that depend on the entries made during quick commissioning.  
p3900 = 1 initially includes a parameter reset (factory setting, the same as p0970
= 1) for all parameters of the drive object; however, without overwriting the entries
made during the quick commissioning.  
The interconnections of PROFIBUS PZD telegram selection (p0922) and the interconnections
via p0700, p1000 and p1500 are re-established and all of the dependent motor, open-loop
and control-loop control parameters are calculated (corresponding to p0340 = 1).  
p3900 = 2 includes the restoration of the interconnections of PROFIBUS PZD telegram
selection (p0922) and the interconnections via p0700, p1000 and p1500 and the calculations
corresponding to p0340 = 1.  
p3900 = 3 only includes the calculations associated with the motor, open-loop and
closed-loop control parameters corresponding to p0340 = 1.

**Value:**
  
0:
No quick parameterization  
1:
Quick parameterization after parameter reset  
2:
Quick parameterization (only) for BICO and motor parameters  
3:
Quick parameterization for motor parameters (only)

**Notice:**
  
After the value has been modified, no further parameter modifications can be made
and the status is shown in r3996. Modifications can be made again when r3996 = 0.

**Note:**
  
When the calculations have been completed, p3900 and p0010 are automatically reset
to a value of zero.  
When calculating motor, open-loop and closed-loop control parameters (such as for
p0340 = 1) parameters associated with a selected Siemens catalog motor are not overwritten.

### p3981 Acknowledge drive object faults

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8060 |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to acknowledge all active faults of a drive object.

**Notice:**
  
Safety messages cannot be acknowledged using this parameter.

**Note:**
  
Parameter should be set from 0 to 1 to acknowledge.  
After acknowledgment, the parameter is automatically reset to 0.

### p3985 Master control mode selection

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Setpoints | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the mode to change over the master control / LOCAL mode.

**Value:**
  
0:
Change master control for STW1.0 = 0  
1:
Change master control in operation

**Danger:**
  
When changing the master control in operation, the drive can manifest undesirable
behavior - e.g. it can accelerate up to another setpoint.

### p3990 Sequentially resets drive parameters

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**All groups | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 300 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Description:**
  
Parameter to internally calculate subsequent (follow-on) parameters after a factory
setting was carried out.  
The parameter is then reset back to 0.

**Value:**
  
0:
Inactive  
1:
Start a parameter reset  
3:
Start download of volatile parameters from RAM  
5:
Starts a safety parameter reset  
6:
Start reset non-safety/safety parameters  
10:
Start loading parameters saved with p0971 = 10  
11:
Start loading parameters saved with p0971 = 11  
12:
Start loading parameters saved with p0971 = 12  
30:
Start loading delivery condition saved with p0971 = 30  
100:
Start a BICO interconnection reset  
300:
Only Siemens internal

**Dependency:**
  
This parameter is called when first commissioning the system and also for factory
settings if this has been initiated via p0970 or p3900.  
  
Refer to:
p3900

### r3996[0...1] Parameter write inhibit status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**All objects | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Description:**
  
Displays whether writing to parameters is inhibited.  
r3996[0] = 0:  
Parameter write not inhibited.  
0 &lt; r3996[0] &lt; 100:  
Parameter write inhibited. The value shows how the calculations are progressing.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Progress calculations  
[
1]:
Cause

**Note:**
  
For index [1]:  
Only for internal Siemens troubleshooting.

### r4021[0...n] CO: PSA4 digital inputs terminal actual value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9982, 9987, 9988 |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Displays the actual value of the digital inputs/outputs of the Power Stack Adapter
(PSA4).  
This means that the actual input signal can be checked at terminal DI x prior to switching
from the simulation mode (p4095.x = 1) to the terminal mode (p4095.x = 0).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | DI 0 (X322.8) gate supply voltage (GSV) faulted | Yes | No | - |
| 01 | DI 1 (X322.5) cabinet door opened | Yes | No | - |
| 02 | DI 2 (X322.4) | Low | High | - |
| 03 | DI 3 (X322.1) | Low | High | - |
| 04 | DI 4 (X323.8) (status fan 1 on) | Low | High | - |
| 05 | DI 5 (X323.5) (status fan 2 on) | Low | High | - |
| 06 | DI 6 (X323.4) | Low | High | - |
| 07 | DI 7 (X323.1) | Low | High | - |
| 16 | DI/DO 0 (X321.13) (protective breaker GSV tripped) | Low | High | - |
| 17 | DI/DO 1 (X321.12) (protective breaker SITOP 24 V tripped) | Low | High | - |
| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) (protective breaker PSA tripped) | Low | High | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) | Low | High | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) | Low | High | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) | Low | High | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) | Low | High | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) (prot. breaker anticondens. heating tripped) | Low | High | - |

**Note:**
  
DI: Digital Input  
The significance of the inputs with the associated descriptive text is defined.  
The inputs with the text set in brackets or without text can be freely interconnected;
the text in brackets designates the factory setting.

| 02 | DI 2 (X322.4) | Low | High | - |
| 03 | DI 3 (X322.1) | Low | High | - |

**Note:**
  
DI: Digital Input  
The significance of the inputs with the associated descriptive text is defined.  
The inputs with the text set in brackets or without text can be freely interconnected;
the text in brackets designates the factory setting.

### r4047[0...n] PSA4 digital outputs status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9983, 9987, 9988 |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Displays the status of the digital outputs of Power Stack Adapter (PSA4).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 08 | DO 0 (X331.15) command open circuit breaker | Yes | No | - |
| 09 | DO 1 (X331.14) reserved | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | DO 2 (X331.11) enable close make-proof grounding switch | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | DO 3 (X331.10) (command door release on) | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | DO 4 (X331.7) (command fan 1 on) | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | DO 5 (X331.6) (command fan 2 on) | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | DO 6 (X331.3) | Low | High | - |
| 15 | DO 7 (X331.2) | Low | High | - |
| 16 | DI/DO 0 (X321.13) | Low | High | - |
| 17 | DI/DO 1 (X321.12) | Low | High | - |
| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) | Low | High | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) | Low | High | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) | Low | High | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) | Low | High | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) | Low | High | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) | Low | High | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p4028, p4048

**Note:**
  
Inversion using p4048 has been taken into account.  
DO: Digital Output  
DI/DO: Bidirectional Digital Input/Output  
The significance of the outputs with the associated descriptive text is defined.  
The outputs with the text set in brackets or without text can be freely interconnected;
the text in brackets designates the factory setting.

### p4048[0...n] PSA4 invert digital outputs

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9983, 9987, 9988 |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Setting to invert the signals at the digital outputs of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA4).  
The inversion is only effective for the freely interconnectable digital outputs.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 10 | Invert DO 2 (X331.11) | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Invert DO 3 (X331.10) | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Invert DO 4 (X331.7) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 13 | Invert DO 5 (X331.6) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 14 | Invert DO 6 (X331.3) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 15 | Invert DO 7 (X331.2) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 16 | Invert DI/DO 0 (X321.13) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 17 | Invert DI/DO 1 (X321.12) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 18 | Invert DI/DO 2 (X321.11) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 19 | Invert DI/DO 3 (X321.10) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 20 | Invert DI/DO 4 (X321.6) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 21 | Invert DI/DO 5 (X321.5) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 22 | Invert DI/DO 6 (X321.4) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 23 | Invert DI/DO 7 (X321.3) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |

**Note:**
  
DO: Digital Output  
DI/DO: Bidirectional Digital Input/Output  
The inversion is only effective for the freely interconnectable digital outputs.  
When the signal is configured as input, then the associated bit in p4048 has no function.

| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 03 | DI 3 (X322.1) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 04 | DI 4 (X323.8) (status fan 1 on) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 05 | DI 5 (X323.5) (status fan 2 on) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 06 | DI 6 (X323.4) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 07 | DI 7 (X323.1) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 16 | DI/DO 0 (X321.13) (protective breaker GSV tripped) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 17 | DI/DO 1 (X321.12) (protective breaker SITOP 24 V tripped) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) (protective breaker PSA tripped) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) (prot. breaker anticondens. heating tripped) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |

**Dependency:**
  
The setpoint for the input signals is specified using p4096.  
  
Refer to:
p4096

**Note:**
  
This parameter is not saved when data is backed-up (p0971, p0977).  
DI: Digital Input  
The significance of the inputs with the associated descriptive text is defined.  
The inputs with the text set in brackets or without text can be freely interconnected;
the text in brackets designates the factory setting.

### p4096[0...n] PSA4 digital inputs simulation mode setpoint

| --- | --- | --- |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the setpoint of the input signals in the simulation mode for the digital inputs
of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA4).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 03 | DI 3 (X322.1) | Low | High | - |
| 04 | DI 4 (X323.8) (status fan 1 on) | Low | High | - |
| 05 | DI 5 (X323.5) (status fan 2 on) | Low | High | - |
| 06 | DI 6 (X323.4) | Low | High | - |
| 07 | DI 7 (X323.1) | Low | High | - |
| 16 | DI/DO 0 (X321.13) (protective breaker GSV tripped) | Low | High | - |
| 17 | DI/DO 1 (X321.12) (protective breaker SITOP 24 V tripped) | Low | High | - |
| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) (protective breaker PSA tripped) | Low | High | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) | Low | High | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) | Low | High | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) | Low | High | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) | Low | High | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) (prot. breaker anticondens. heating tripped) | Low | High | - |

**Dependency:**
  
The simulation of a digital input is selected using p4095.  
  
Refer to:
p4095

**Note:**
  
This parameter is not saved when data is backed-up (p0971, p0977).  
DI: Digital Input  
The significance of the inputs with the associated descriptive text is defined.  
The inputs with the text set in brackets or without text can be freely interconnected;
the text in brackets designates the factory setting.

r4955[0...8]: Identifier of Technology Extension 1  
r4955[9...17]: Identifier of Technology Extension 2, ...

r4950, r4951, r4952, p4956, r4957, r4958, r4959, r4960

### p5954 BI: Extended buffering line fault suppression signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |

### p5955[0...1] Line freq. deviation for fast line supply diagnostics for BLD

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORSL, A_INFMV, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [%] | 10.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 2.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the deviation for fast diagnostics of the line supply recovery.  
For fast diagnostics of line recovery after a line interruption, the actual value
of the line frequency is compared with a reference value saved before the interruption.
If the deviation is less than the value set in p5955, then it is assumed that the
line is now fault-free.  
The fast line diagnostics is used to speed-up pulse enable using the buffering function
(if this is required) after a short line interruption.  
If p5955 = 0, then fast line diagnostics is not used.  
When setting p5955, the following should be observed:  
The value should correspond as close as possible to the actual deviation of the line
frequency from the average value. However, the actual deviation can manifest fluctuations
and should therefore always be regularly checked.  
Generally, both indices can be assigned the same value.  
When required, a fine setting can be made using the indices.  
For index [0]:  
As long as the line is fault-free, the actual frequency is saved as reference value.
If the deviation exceeds the value of p5955[0], then a line fault is assumed. The
last saved value is kept as reference value. The system now waits for the line to
recover.  
For index [1]:  
The line frequency is compared with the reference value. If the deviation lies below
p5955[1], then it is assumed that the line supply has recovered.  
Please note the following:  
If the value is selected too high, then it is possible that a fault-free line is diagnosed
too early and the pulses are enabled at the incorrect time.  
If the value is selected too low, the diagnostics can fail and be ineffective.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Diagnostics before line supply interruption  
[
1]:
Diagnostics of line return

**Note:**
  
BLD: Buffering brief line dips

### p6026[0...7] BI: PU door release enable signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9827 |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0...6 ] 1   [ 7 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal sources for the electromagnetic door release enable.  
The signals that are read in are ANDed with the internal states such as 'U=0', 'pulse
inhibit' and 'circuit breaker open' and output at Power Stack Adapter output DO3.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Internal 1  
[
1]:
Internal 2  
[
2]:
Internal 3  
[
3]:
External 1  
[
4]:
External 2  
[
5]:
External 3  
[
6]:
Static  
[
7]:
Temporary

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6027

### r6035 CO: Cooling unit cooling liquid temperature actual value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

### p6060[0...1] BI: External command switch in power unit cooling signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), VECTORGL, VECTOR3P (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), VECTORM2C (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), A_INFMV (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), B_INFMV (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), A_INFM2C (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), BMM2C (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the external command "switch on power unit cooling".  
BI: p6060 = 0 signal  
If there is no internal switch-on signal, then the power unit cooling is switched
off.  
Exceptions:  
- when the run-on time (p0264) is appropriately set.  
- when fault F49156 occurs.  
BI: p6060 = 1 signal  
The drive converter cooling is switched on.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Redundancy subsystem 1  
[
1]:
Redundancy subsystem 2

**Note:**
  
For redundancy, the cooling units of both subsystems are switched on using their own
index

### r6072[0...n] CO: Power semiconductor test valve status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 8146 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output of the defective power semiconductors after the test
has been executed.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Phase U status valve 1 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 01 | Phase U status valve 2 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 02 | Phase U status valve 3 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 03 | Phase U status valve 4 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 04 | Phase V status valve 1 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 05 | Phase V status valve 2 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 06 | Phase V status valve 3 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 07 | Phase V status valve 4 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 08 | Phase W status valve 1 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 09 | Phase W status valve 2 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 10 | Phase W status valve 3 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 11 | Phase W status valve 4 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 16 | Phase U status diode 1 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 17 | Phase U status diode 2 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 18 | Phase V status diode 1 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 19 | Phase V status diode 2 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 20 | Phase W status diode 1 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 21 | Phase W status diode 2 | Okay | Defect | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r3402, p6070  
Refer to:
F30112, F30122

**Note:**
  
As a result of a defective power semiconductor, it is possible that other power semiconductors
cannot be tested. These are also displayed as defect.

### p6085 Power semiconductor test voltage limit DC link charged

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8146 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 50.0 [%] | 100.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 80.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the upper DC link voltage limit when testing power semiconductors.  
The DC link is considered to be charged if the DC link voltage is above this limit.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6086, p6626

**Note:**
  
The voltage limit "DC link charged" refers to the voltage threshold (40 V), below
which the power semiconductor test is started.

### p6086 Power semiconductor test voltage limit DC link discharged

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the lower DC link voltage limit when testing power semiconductors.  
The DC link is considered to be discharged if the DC link voltage is below this limit.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6085, p6626

**Note:**
  
The voltage limit "DC link discharged" refers to the voltage threshold (40 V), below
which the power semiconductor test is started.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for feedback signal 'premagnetization completed'. The command
to switch on premagnetization is output in the control commands via r6221 bit 10.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6221

**Note:**
  
The input is not evaluated for an interconnection to '0'. The input side circuit breaker
is then closed without premagnetizing the transformer.

### p6219 Transformer inrush delay time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [s] | 100.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 5.0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the delay time for the transformer inrush.  
After the delay time has expired, the line supply monitoring/line supply control is
enabled.

**Note:**
  
If a Voltage Sensing Module (VSM) is being used, after the delay time for the inrush
has expired the system waits for the PLL / line monitor ready signal. This the reason
that the pulse enable can be additionally delayed by up to 4 seconds.

### p6296[0...2] Cooling unit water flow thresholds

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9798 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), VECTOR3P (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), VECTORM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), A_INFMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), B_INFMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), A_INFM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), BMM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -75.00 [ltr/min] | 375.00 [ltr/min] | [ 0 ] -1.00 [ltr/min]  [ 1 ] 160.00 [ltr/min]  [ 2 ] 5.00 [ltr/min] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the thresholds for monitoring the water flow.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Water flow plausibility threshold  
[
1]:
Water flow alarm threshold  
[
2]:
Water flow hysteresis

**Note:**
  
The following generally applies:  
The factory setting value is appropriately set depending on the equipment description
data.  
For index [0]:  
If the flux monitoring is active (fault threshold (device description data) ! =0),
the following applies to the plausibility threshold setting:  
Wire breakage threshold (equipment description data) &lt; plausibility threshold &lt; -1
liter.  
If the plausibility threshold is set lower than the wire breakage threshold, then
the value is limited to the wire breakage threshold.  
For index [1]:  
The following applies to the alarm threshold setting:  
Hysteresis &lt; alarm threshold &gt; fault threshold (equipment description data) + hysteresis  
If the alarm threshold is set lower than the fault threshold + hysteresis, then the
value is limited to the fault threshold + hysteresis.  
For index [2]:  
The following applies to the hysteresis setting:  
0 &lt; hysteresis &lt; 20 liters

### p6298[0...1] Cooling unit differential pressure min value alarm thresh

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( ) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9799 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), VECTOR3P (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), VECTORM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), A_INFMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), B_INFMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), A_INFM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), BMM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.1 [bar] | 10.0 [bar] | [ 0 ] 2.0 [bar]  [ 1 ] 0.3 [bar] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the alarm threshold/hysteresis for the minimum differential pressure.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Liquid differential pressure alarm threshold  
[
1]:
Liquid differential pressure hysteresis

**Note:**
  
The following applies to the alarm threshold setting:  
Alarm threshold &gt; fault threshold (hardware description data)  
If the alarm threshold is set lower than the fault threshold, then the value is limited
to the fault threshold (from the hardware description data) + hysteresis (p6298.1).  
The following generally applies:  
The factory setting value is appropriately set depending on the hardware description
data.  
The following applies to the hysteresis setting:  
The hysteresis must be greater than 0 and less than or equal to 6 bar.

### p6299[0...1] Power unit cooling input temperature alarm threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( ) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9797 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), A_INFMV, B_INFMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit, Fan), A_INFM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), BMM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [°C] | 100.0 [°C] | [ 0 ] 40.0 [°C]  [ 1 ] 1.0 [°C] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the alarm threshold for the maximum intake temperature for liquid cooling or
intake temperature for air cooling.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Temperature alarm threshold  
[
1]:
Temperature hysteresis

**Note:**
  
The following generally applies:  
The factory setting value is appropriately set depending on the equipment description
data.  
For index [0]:  
The following applies to the alarm threshold setting:  
20 °C &lt; alarm threshold &lt; fault threshold (equipment description data) - hysteresis.  
If the alarm threshold is set higher than the fault threshold - hysteresis, then the
value is limited to the fault threshold - hysteresis.  
For index [1]:  
The following applies to the hysteresis setting:  
0 °C &lt; hysteresis &lt; 5 °C

### p6411 Line transformer relative short-cct voltage uk correction factor

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 10.000 [%] | 1000.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.000 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the correction factor of the relative short-circuit voltage uk of the line transformer.

**Recommend.:**
  
Enter the result in r3411 from the identification via p3410 = 2 into this parameter.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3410, r3411, p6520

### p6417 Infeed rotating field generator operating mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the operating mode for the rotating field generator.

**Value:**
  
0:
Rotating field generator off  
1:
Rotating field generator rotating voltage field  
2:
Rotating field generator, rotating voltage field, synchronized  
3:
Rotating field generator, rotating current field, controlled

**Danger:**
  
Only qualified service personnel may activate this test mode during the routine check.
It is not permissible that the drive converter is connected and operated from the
line supply.

**Note:**
  
The rotating field generator is activated using p6650 = 20.

### p6419 Infeed rotating field generator ramp duration frequency

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the rise time for the frequency ramp from 0 ... f_rated (p0211).

**Note:**
  
For safety reasons, the frequency ramp is limited to a minimum time of 0.5 s. The
ramp can be bypassed by setting the starting value (p6894) and final value (p6892)
of the frequency to the same value.

### p6420[0...1] Phase shift input voltage VSM to the drive converter

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6735 |
| **Object:**VECTORSL, A_INFMV, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**All groups | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -180.000 [°] | 179.900 [°] | [ 0 ] 0.000 [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the phase shift between the synchronizing voltage measured by the Voltage Sensing
Module (VSM) and the actual drive converter input voltage.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Phase A/B/C, system 1  
[
1]:
Phase D/E/F, system 2

**Warning:**
  
Switching-in with a significantly incorrectly parameterized offset angle (&gt; 5 °) can
cause a peak current intervention and / or triggering the crowbar thyristor.

**Caution:**
  
If this parameter is changed in the "ready for operation" state and if a synchronizing
voltage is already available at the VSM, under certain circumstances, a line supply
fault can be signaled. When this occurs for the first time after changing the parameter,
the fault can be ignored and acknowledged.

**Note:**
  
This phase shift must be determined when commissioning the system.  
Example:  
If the converter input voltage (= secondary side voltage of the power transformer)
lags the synchronizing voltage measured by the VSM by 30 °, then p6420 should be set
to -30°.

| --- | --- | --- |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 50.000 [%] | 200.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.000 [%] |

### p6422 Line supply voltage rotating field direction

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to reverse the rotating field direction of the synchronizing voltage system
measured by the Voltage Sensing Module (VSM).

**Value:**
  
0:
Rotating field direction positive  
1:
Rotating field negative

**Warning:**
  
Only use in an emergency if it is not possible to correct the wiring. Extreme caution
must be applied in this case when measuring the phase shift (p6420).

**Note:**
  
Allows the rotating field direction to be adapted if there is inconsistency in the
wiring.

## SINAMICS Parameter ACTIVE INFEED CONTROLMV 06432 - 06632

SINAMICS Parameter ACTIVE INFEED CONTROLMV 06432 - 06632

### p6432 Infeed duration changeover to instantaneous values

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORSL, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [ms] | 1000.000 [ms] | [ 0 ] 0.000 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the time during which after a line supply fault the unsmoothed line voltage actual
values are used for voltage precontrol of the infeed.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0286  
Refer to:
A06106, F06107

**Notice:**
  
This parameter should only be adjusted for acute and difficult line supply problems.

**Note:**
  
This mechanism is not effective for p6432 = 0 ms.

### r6440 Infeed identification phase shift U_input VSM/converter

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [°] | - [°] | [ ] - [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the identified offset angle between the input voltage of the VSM and the
connection voltage of the power unit.  
For a positive rotational field (clockwise rotation, positive frequency), the result
should be subtracted from the value in p6420; for a negative rotational filed (counter-clockwise
rotation, negative frequency), it should be added.  
The result should be entered into p6420.  
Example:  
r6440 = 30  
p6420 = 0  
r0066 (for test mode 1) or r6445 (for test mode 8) &gt; 0 (positive frequency, clockwise
rotational field)  
--&gt; p6420 = 0 - 30 = -30

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0066, p3410, p6420

**Note:**
  
In test mode 8 (p3410 = 8), the identified offset angle should also be observed by
tracing p6444[0], since the individual results are scattered.  
In test mode 1 (p3410 = 1), the frequency in r0066 is displayed; in test mode 8 (p3410
= 8) it is displayed in r6445.

### p6443 Infeed identification switch-on time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [ms] | 1000.000 [ms] | [ 0 ] 0.000 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the switch-on duration of the pulses when activating the identification for identification
type 1.  
This function serves as a commissioning help.  
After the set time expires, Alarm A06405 is output and the pulses are automatically
inhibited. To exit this state, the operating enable p0852 must be canceled.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3410  
Refer to:
A06405

**Note:**
  
For a value of 0, the identification is continuously performed; the pulses are not
automatically inhibited.  
A value not equal to 0 specifies the time from synchronization up to when the pulses
are automatically inhibited.

### r6444[0...3] CO: Infeed identification monitor signals

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays significant signals (percentage values) during the individual identification
modes.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3410

**Note:**
  
Mode 1: Identifies the phase angle and the gain of the measured value acquisition  
[0] &gt; Line supply voltage - 1.0  
[1] &gt; DC link voltages actual value - setpoint  
[2] &gt; Active current actual value  
[3] &gt; Modulation depth - rated modulation depth  
Mode 2: Identifies the transformer short-circuit voltage uk  
[0] &gt; Supplementary setpoint, reactive power  
[1] &gt; DC link voltages actual value - setpoint  
[2] &gt; Modulation depth - rated modulation depth  
[3] &gt; Reactive current actual value  
Mode 3: Identifies the DC link capacitance  
[0] &gt; Supplementary setpoint, DC link voltage  
[1] &gt; DC link voltages actual value - setpoint  
[2] &gt; Active current actual value  
[3] &gt; Reactive current actual value  
Mode 4: Squarewave function to the reactive power supplementary setpoint  
[0] &gt; Supplementary setpoint, reactive power  
[1] &gt; Reactive current actual value  
[2] &gt; Active current actual value  
[3] &gt; DC link voltages actual value - setpoint  
Mode 5: Squarewave function to the DC link voltage setpoint  
[0] &gt; Supplementary setpoint, DC link voltage  
[1] &gt; DC link voltages actual value - setpoint  
[2] &gt; Active current actual value  
[3] &gt; Reactive current actual value  
Mode 6: Squarewave function to the DC link center potential setpoint  
[0] &gt; DC link center potential setpoint  
[1] &gt; DC link center potential actual value  
[2] &gt; Active current actual value  
[3] &gt; Reactive current actual value  
Mode 7: Signals to evaluate the precontrol  
[0] &gt; DC link voltages actual value - setpoint  
[1] &gt; Active current actual value  
[2] &gt; Reactive current actual value  
[3] &gt; DC link center potential actual value  
Mode 8: Determination of the offset angle and direction of rotation  
[0] &gt; Offset angle between input voltage VSM and converter  
[1] &gt; Rotational field frequency converter input voltage  
[2] &gt; DC link voltage actual value  
[3] &gt; DC link center potential actual value

### p6447[0...1] Infeed identification Vdc limits

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 103.0 [%] | 115.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 105.0 [%]  [ 1 ] 115.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the DC link voltage limits for test mode 8 (p3410 = 8).  
[0]: Lower DC link voltage limit.  
[1]: Upper DC link voltage limit.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3410  
Refer to:
A06407

**Note:**
  
In test mode 8 (p3410 = 8), the identification is aborted when the upper DC link voltage
threshold is reached and restarted when the value falls below the lower threshold.
If the line connection voltage is too high, the lower threshold may have to be modified.
The difference between upper and lower threshold must be at least 2 percent.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

### p6475 Center potential controller Kp

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8947 |
| **Object:**A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [%] | 1000.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.000 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the proportional gain (Kp) of the center potential controller.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6480

**Note:**
  
The proportional gain (Kp) is approx. 100% if the DC link differential voltage is
scaled to the rated value of the total DC link voltage.  
p6475 = 0:  
The internal proportional controller is deactivated.

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 70.000 [%] | 99.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 85.000 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the modulation depth threshold for the tracking of the proportional gain (Kp)
of the center potential controller.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6476

### p6478 Center pot. controller DC link center potential, smoothing time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8947 |

| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 5.000 [ms] | 200.000 [ms] | [ 0 ] 10.000 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

### r6479[0...n] CO: Center potential controller manipulated variable

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 6718 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the manipulated variable of the center potential
controller.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6474

**Note:**
  
This value refers to the angular pre-rotation (advance) specified in p6474 and is
limited to +/-95 %.  
This value is displayed without any dimensions when transferred via the CU link (e.g.
master-slave circuit).

### p6480 CI: Center potential controller manipulated variable external

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6717 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**PERCENT | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for an external manipulated variable of the center potential
controller.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6475, r6479

**Note:**
  
For a master - slave circuit of infeeds, this input is used to interconnect the center
potential manipulated variable (r6479) of the master to the slave.  
p6475 = 0:  
The internal proportional controller is deactivated.

### p6484 Infeed DC link voltage control damping

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8940 |
| **Object:**A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 10.000 [%] | 200.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 60.000 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the damping of the DC link voltage control.

**Note:**
  
Lower values increase the dynamic response but also the tendency of the control loop
to oscillate (instability).

Sets the damping of the reactive power control.

**Note:**
  
Lower values increase the dynamic response but also the tendency of the control loop
to oscillate (instability).

### p6488 Infeed de-coupling active/reactive power

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8940 |
| **Object:**A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 10.000 [%] | 300.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.000 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the adaptation of the de-coupling angle between the active and reactive current
internally calculated from the controller and control loop parameters.

**Note:**
  
This parameter can be optimized using identification mode 5 so that the coupling between
active and reactive current is minimal.

### p6489 Infeed precontrol voltage active/reactive components

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8942 |
| **Object:**A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [%] | 300.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.000 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the adaptation of the precontrolled reactive voltage component.

**Note:**
  
The active current, provided by the power precontrol results in a voltage component
in the reactive axis. This voltage is also precontrolled and weighted with this factor.

### p6490 Infeed reactive current limit

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [%] | 200.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.000 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum total current to limit the reactive current setpoint.

**Note:**
  
The active current, derived from the precontrolled active power forms, together with
the reactive current setpoint, the summed (total) current. If this exceeds the limit
defined here, the reactive current setpoint is limited. The active current setpoint
is not limited, as this would affect the DC link voltage.

### p6501[0...62] External digital message, component assignment

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8030 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Messages | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 32767 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the component assignment of the external digital messages.  
The messages are issued on a component for component basis.  
p6501 = 0:  
The message is always issued.  
p6501 = A, A &gt; 0:  
The message belongs to component A and is issued when component A is active.  
More information is provided in p7100 ... p7108.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
A49000 (F) - BO: r6509.0  
[
1]:
F49001 (A) - BO: r6509.1  
[
2]:
A49002 (F) - BO: r6509.2  
[
3]:
F49003 (A) - BO: r6509.3  
[
4]:
A49004 (F) - BO: r6509.4  
[
5]:
F49005 (A) - BO: r6509.5

|  |  |  |

| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8031 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Messages | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source (connector output) for the signal of the external analog messages.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
A49090 (F) - BO: r6506.0  
[
1]:
A49091 (F) - BO: r6506.1  
[
2]:
A49092 (F) - BO: r6506.2  
[
3]:
F49093 (A) - BO: r6506.3  
[
4]:
F49094 (A) - BO: r6506.4

5]:
F49095 (A) - BO: r6506.5  
[
6]:

7]:
F49097 (A) - BO: r6506.7

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6504, p6505, r6506, p6508

**Notice:**
  
The fault response OFF1 is assigned as standard in faults. This fault response can
only be executed in full if the delay time setting for OFF1 (p3490) is greater than
the ramp-down time for the drive.

**Note:**
  
Threshold value: p6504[x]  
Hysteresis: p6505[x]  
Enable: p6508[x]

### p6507[0...7] External analog message component assignment

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8031 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Messages | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 32767 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the component assignment of the external analog messages.  
The messages are issued on a component for component basis.  
p6507 = 0:  
The message is always issued.  
p6507 = A, A &gt; 0:  
The message belongs to component A and is issued when component A is active.  
More information is provided in p7100 ... p7108.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
A49090 (F) - BO: r6506.0  
[
1]:
A49091 (F) - BO: r6506.1  
[
2]:
A49092 (F) - BO: r6506.2  
[
3]:
F49093 (A) - BO: r6506.3  
[
4]:
F49094 (A) - BO: r6506.4  
[
5]:
F49095 (A) - BO: r6506.5  
[
6]:
F49096 (A) - BO: r6506.6  
[
7]:
F49097 (A) - BO: r6506.7

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6503, p6504, p6505, r6506, p6508

### p6522 Line transformer rated primary current

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [Arms] | 5000.000 [Arms] | [ 0 ] 420.000 [Arms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the primary rated current of the line transformer.

**Note:**
  
For a three-winding or four-winding transformer (1 primary winding system and 2 or
3 secondary winding systems), normally, the resulting total current is specified while
the relative short-circuit voltage refers to an individual system. In this case, the
primary-side rated current, specified in the data sheet, should be halved or divided
by three.

### p6531 Precharging two-level control enable

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9833, 9834 |
| **Object:**A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the enable signal of the two-level control of the precharging.

**Value:**
  
0:
Not enabled  
1:
Enabled

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6621, r6622, p6623

**Note:**
  
The maximum activation time of the two-level control is defined in the hardware description
data.  
When the two-level control is enabled (p6531 = 1) the following applies:  
The DC link voltage is controlled between the upper and lower thresholds (p6622[0,
1]).  
The DC link voltage actual value is set using p6623[0, 1].

in the indices.

F01275

An ASCII table (excerpt) can be found, for example, in the appendix to the List Manual.  
GUID: Globally Unique IDentifier

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the control signals of the precharging.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Precharging on command from the sequence control  
[
1]:
Precharging circuit is switched on  
[
2]:
Precharging transformer temperature monitor OK  
[
3]:
Precharging off command external  
[
4]:
Precharging on command external  
[
5]:
Precharging on command from power semiconductor test  
[
6]:
Precharging ON command for DSM update  
[
7]:
Precharging fault, precharging converter

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6221

**Note:**
  
For index [2]:  
If there is no temperature monitor, then interconnect to a fixed value.  
For index [3]:  
When the binector input is interconnected, precharging is switched-on independent
of the magnitude of the precharging threshold.  
For index [5]:  
This index is only used for test operation of the power semiconductors (p6650 = 4).
The pre-charging setpoint refers to p6626.  
For index [6]:  
Keep the factory setting.  
For index [7]:  
Keep the factory setting.

### r6571.0...8 CO/BO: Precharging control and status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9833, 9834 |
| **Object:**A_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Displays the control word and status word for precharging.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Activate precharging | OFF | ON | - |
| 01 | Precharging active | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Precharging complete | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Switch-on precharging power semiconductor test | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Switch off precharging external | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Precharging switched on feedback | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Temperature monitor precharging transformer responded | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Precharging fault, precharging converter | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6570, p6623  
Refer to:
F05210, F30060, F30063

### p6583[0...3] PSA analog inputs selection

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0001 bin   [ 1 ] 0001 bin   [ 2 ] 0000 bin   [ 3 ] 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to select the available analog inputs of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
The parameter is bit-coded.  
Each bit corresponds to a PSA:  
p6583[x] = 0000 0000 0000 dcba, d: PSA3, c: PSA2, b: PSA1, a: PSA0  
A bit that is set signifies that the analog input on the PSA in question is activated.  
Example:  
AI 0 should be selected from PSA0 and AI 1 from PSA1.  
p6583 should be set as follows:  
p6583[0] = 0000 0000 0000 0001  
p6583[1] = 0000 0000 0000 0010

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Analog input 0  
[
1]:
Analog input 1  
[
2]:
Analog input 2  
[
3]:
Analog input 3

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | PSA0: CHANNEL SEL | No evaluation | Evaluation | - |
| 01 | PSA1: CHANNEL SEL | No evaluation | Evaluation | - |
| 02 | PSA2: CHANNEL SEL | No evaluation | Evaluation | - |
| 03 | PSA3: CHANNEL SEL | No evaluation | Evaluation | - |

**Note:**
  
The following applies when several analog inputs are selected:  
The channel from the first active PSA is evaluated.  
The evaluation has no influence on the corresponding signal monitoring.

### p6584[0...3] PSA temperature inputs selection

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0001 bin   [ 1 ] 0000 bin   [ 2 ] 0000 bin   [ 3 ] 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the available temperature inputs of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA) for selection.  
The parameter is bit-coded. Each bit corresponds to a PSA:  
p6584[x] = 0000 0000 0000 dcba, d: PSA3, c: PSA2, b: PSA1, a: PSA0  
A bit that is set signifies that the temperature input of the PSA involved is activated.  
Example:  
TEMP 1 should be selected from PSA0 and TEMP 0 from PSA1.  
p6584 should be set as follows:  
p6584[0] = 0000 0000 0000 0010  
p6584[1] = 0000 0000 0000 0001

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Temperature input 0  
[
1]:
Temperature input 1  
[
2]:
Temperature input 2  
[
3]:
Temperature input 3

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | PSA0: CHANNEL SEL | No evaluation | Evaluation | - |
| 01 | PSA1: CHANNEL SEL | No evaluation | Evaluation | - |
| 02 | PSA2: CHANNEL SEL | No evaluation | Evaluation | - |
| 03 | PSA3: CHANNEL SEL | No evaluation | Evaluation | - |

**Note:**
  
The following applies when several temperature inputs are selected:  
The highest measured value is used.

### r6595[0...n] Cabinet 1 auxiliary fan 1 operating hours

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P (Aux fan ctrl), VECTORM2C (Aux fan ctrl), VECTORDM (Aux fan ctrl), A_INFMV (Aux fan ctrl), A_INFM2C (Aux fan ctrl), BMM2C (Aux fan ctrl) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [h] | - [h] | [ ] - [h] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Display of the operating hours of fan 1 in cabinet 1.  
Prerequisite:  
The operating hours were saved in a non-volatile fashion on the Power Stack Adapters
(PSA).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p17750, p17751, p17752

**Note:**
  
This parameter is only relevant for firmware versions lower than V4.8.  
When upgrading from versions lower than V4.8, then the displayed value can be entered
in p17750, p17750 and p17752.  
The operating hours currently used in the software are saved in a non-volatile fashion
on the NVRAM of the Control Unit. They can be read and changed using parameters p17750,
p17751 and p17752.

(PSA).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p17750, p17751, p17752

**Note:**
  
This parameter is only relevant for firmware versions lower than V4.8.  
When upgrading from versions lower than V4.8, then the displayed value can be entered
in p17750, p17750 and p17752.  
The operating hours currently used in the software are saved in a non-volatile fashion
on the NVRAM of the Control Unit. They can be read and changed using parameters p17750,
p17751 and p17752.

### p6620 DC link precharging test operation setpoint

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9834 |
| **Object:**A_INFMV, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [%] | 100.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 10.000 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the setpoint to precharge the DC link for test operation.  
The setpoint for the precharging refers to the setpoint of the DC link voltage (p3510).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3510, p6650

**Note:**
  
The pre-charging test mode is set using p6650 = 3.  
The two-level control (p6531 = 1) only functions for a setpoint above 10 %, as, at
this value, the lower threshold is greater than zero (p6622[1] &gt; 0).

[
1]:

### p6623[0...1] CI: DC link precharging actual value signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9833, 9834 |
| **Object:**A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the DC link voltage actual value of the precharging.  
When precharging is active (r6571.1 = 1), the following applies:  
The actual value of the DC link voltage via signal source with index 0 is evaluated.  
When precharging is not active (r6571.1 = 0), the following applies:  
The actual value of the DC link voltage via signal source with index 1 is evaluated.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Actual value precharging active  
[
1]:
Actual value precharging inactive

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6571

**Note:**
  
A distinction is made between active and inactive precharging if index 1 was connected.
Otherwise, index number 0 is always valid.

### r6625[0...4] DC link voltages status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [V] | - [V] | [ ] - [V] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the status of the DC link voltages of the power unit.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
DC link voltage  
[
1]:
Permissible to reduce the DC link voltage  
[
2]:
DC link voltage from the supply voltage (p0210)  
[
3]:
DC link voltage reference  
[
4]:
DC link voltage from the actual supply voltage

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6639

**Note:**
  
For r6625[0]:  
Displays the DC link voltage corresponding with the voltage class of the power unit
(peak rectification).  
For r6625[1]:  
Displays the permissible DC link voltage, after taking into account the installation
altitude.  
For r6625[2]:  
Displays the DC link voltage resulting from the supply voltage (p0210).  
For r6625[3]:  
Display of the reference value of the DC link voltage is the same as r6625[2] for
a diode infeed or r6625[2] * step-up factor when the infeed is active.  
For r6625[4]:  
Displays the estimated (diode infeed) or measured (active infeed) from the DC link
voltage resulting from the actual supply voltage for peak rectification.  
Example:  
In order to explain the parameter indices and their interdependencies, the commissioning
of a fictitious power unit is described as an example. This involves a qualitative
example whose quantitative values are not generally valid.  
Assumption:  
Power unit with voltage class 3300 V, installation altitude 1000 m above sea level,
transformer tap at 3000 V, line supply voltage decreases, over the long term, by 5
%.  
Voltage class 3300 V:  
r6625[0] = sqrt(2) * 3300 V = 4667 V  
Before the installation altitude can be successfully entered into p6649, the line
supply voltage must be set to a lower value using p0210 (e.g. 2000 V).  
After p6649 = 1000 m is entered, the DC link voltage, resulting from the installation
altitude derating is taken into account.  
r6625[1] = sqrt(2) * 3300 V * 0.95 = 4433 V  
A transformer tap is now selected and therefore the supply voltage, whose peak rectification
value lies below r6625[1] (e.g. p0210 = 2800 V). This has the following result:  
r6625[2] = sqrt(2) * 2800 V = 3960 V  
If a drive with diode infeed is being used, the reference value of the DC link voltage
is also assigned this value; for a drive with active infeed, this is weighted with
the step-up factor.  
r6625[3] = sqrt(2) * 2800 V = 3960 V (diode infeed)  
r6625[3] = sqrt(2) * 2800 V * 1.08 = 4277 V (active infeed)  
Now, in operation, over the long term, the line supply voltage decreases by 5%:  
r6625[4] = sqrt(2) * 2800 V * 0.95 = 3762 V

### r6648[0...3] Supply voltage, status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [Vrms] | - [Vrms] | [ ] - [Vrms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the status of the supply voltages of the power unit.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Voltage class  
[
1]:
Reduced input voltage permissible  
[
2]:
Supply voltage (p0210).  
[
3]:
Actual supply voltage

**Note:**
  
For r6648[0]:  
Displays the voltage class of the power unit.  
For r6648[1]:  
Displays the permissible supply voltage, after taking into account the installation
altitude.  
For r6648[2]:  
Displays the line supply voltage setting (p0210).  
For r6648[3]:  
Displays the estimated (diode infeed) or measured (active infeed) actual supply voltage.  
Example:  
In order to explain the parameter indices and their interdependencies, the commissioning
of a fictitious power unit is described as an example. This involves a qualitative
example whose quantitative values are not generally valid.  
Assumption:  
Power unit with voltage class 3300 V, installation altitude 1000 m above sea level,
transformer tap at 3000 V, line supply voltage decreases, over the long term, by 5
%.  
Voltage class 3300 V:  
r6648[0] = 3300 V  
Before the installation altitude can be successfully entered into p6649, the line
supply voltage must be set to a lower value using p0210 (e.g. 2000 V).  
After p6649 = 1000 m is entered, the DC link voltage, resulting from the installation
altitude derating is taken into account.  
r6648[1] = 3300 V * 0.95 = 3135 V  
A transformer tap is now selected and therefore the supply voltage, whose value lies
below r6648[1] (e.g. p0210 = 2800 V). This has the following result:  
r6648[2] = 2800 V  
Now, in operation, over the long term, the line supply voltage decreases by 5%:  
r6648[3] = 2800 V * 0.95 = 2660 V
