### p6650 Sequence control test mode activation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 42 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to activate/deactivate test mode for sequence control.  
For test mode [80]: the CU and DSM should be switched off and on again after the update.

**Value:**
  
0:
Test operation inactive  
2:
Test operation Gate Supply Voltage (GSV)  
3:
Test operation precharging  
4:
Power semiconductor test mode  
6:
Test operation redundant fan  
7:
Test operation auxiliary fan  
10:
Test operation cooling  
12:
Test operation additional auxiliaries  
15:
Test operation line supply monitoring  
20:
Test operation line side switch 1  
30:
Test operation rotating field generator  
31:
Test operation braking chopper  
32:
Test operation crowbar  
40:
Test operation precharging and circuit breaker  
42:
Test mode premagnetization

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0840, p6070, p6651  
Refer to:
A07318

**Warning:**
  
Only appropriately trained service and maintenance personnel may select and execute
the test mode.

**Caution:**
  
Bit r0899.3 (fault present) can be set with delay in the status word due to the message
generation.  
For protection reasons, the "Precharging" test mode is limited from a time perspective,
as operation without cooling is possible.

**Note:**
  
The test is started via binector input p6651 = 0/1 signal.  
For p6650 = 4:  
This test mode only makes sense for IGCT power units. The IGCTs and the center point
diodes are checked by individually controlling the IGCTs.  
For p6650 = 15:  
Activates the line voltage monitoring (function diagram 8961, 8962) when the converter
is ready to be switched on (p3402 = 200).

**Note:**
  
The total duration of the acknowledgment procedure is extended by this time.  
The pulse time has been selected too short if a fault from the external peripherals
must be acknowledged twice.

### r6660.0...5 CO/BO: Switch 0 control signals

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays (binector outputs) the control signals for switch 0.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | "Close" command | Inactive | Active | - |
| 01 | "Open" command | Active | Inactive | - |
| 02 | Command "open undervoltage coil" | Active | Inactive | - |
| 03 | Command "open higher-level switch" | Inactive | Active | - |
| 04 | Software feedback signal closed | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Software feedback signal open | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
For bit 03:  
This signal is set if, as a result of the command "open switch", no feedback signal
was detected and the crowbar thyristor was fired. It can be used to switch off the
plant/system by means of a higher-level switch.

### p6670[0...5] BI: Switch simulation mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source of the simulation mode for the switch.  
When the simulation mode is activated, the feedback signals for the switch are set
the same as the software commands. All monitoring functions are deactivated.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Warning:**
  
Only appropriately trained service and maintenance personnel may select and execute
the simulation mode.

**Note:**
  
BI: p6670[x] = 1 signal: Simulation mode activated  
BI: p6670[x] = 0 signal: Simulation mode deactivated

**Note:**
  
The switch 0 (main switch) is only opened/closed via the sequence control, i.e. binector
input p6671[0] is ineffective.  
BI: p6671[1...4] is used to control optional switches.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

[

[
1]:

### p6675[0...5] Switch open monitoring time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.030 [s] | 20.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.160 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the monitoring time of the feedback signals when opening the switch.  
The feedback signal within the time in p6675 must indicate that the switch has reliably
opened (i.e. bounce can no longer be detected). The monitoring time in p6675 must
be set greater than the time in p6676 as it additionally takes into account the bounce
time of the feedback signal.  
The following applies: p6675 &gt; p6676

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6674, p6676  
Refer to:
F07311

**Danger:**
  
If this monitoring function responds, then it must be assumed that the switch had
not opened. In this case, the drive system is not disconnected from the line supply.

### p6676[0...5] Switch max. opening time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.030 [s] | 20.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.100 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum permissible opening time of the switch.  
The feedback signal must indicate for the first time that the switch has opened within
the time in p6676. The feedback signal must be stable within the time in p6675 (i.e.
bounce can no longer be detected).  
The following applies: p6675 &gt; p6676

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6675  
Refer to:
F07306

**Danger:**
  
The switch used must open within this time in order to protect the drive system.  
Switch with a longer opening time than specified in this parameter cannot be used.

5]:
Switch 5

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Feedback signal switch ready | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Feedback signal switch closed | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Feedback signal switch open | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Feedback signal switch auxiliary voltage 1 OK | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Feedback signal switch auxiliary voltage 2 OK | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Feedback signal no external trip | No | Yes | - |

[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4

5]:
Switch 5

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Not ready | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Feedback signal closed missing | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Feedback signal open missing | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Too slow | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Auxiliary voltage 1 missing | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Auxiliary voltage 2 missing | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Consistency error | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Crowbar thyristor and feedback signal missing | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | External trip | No | Yes | - |

### p6710[0...5] Switch switching alarm threshold lower

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the lower alarm threshold to monitor the number of times that the switch is operated.  
The lower threshold defines the output for the first alarm.

[
0]:

[
1]:
Switch 1

2]:
Switch 2

Switch 3  
[
4]:

to the switching being used.

For p6710 = 0, the monitoring for the lower alarm threshold is switched out.

### p6711[0...5] Switch switching alarm threshold upper

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the upper alarm threshold to monitor the number of times that the switch is operated.  
The upper threshold defines the output for the second alarm.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A07323

**Caution:**
  
After this alarm is output for the first time, the parameters must be set corresponding
to the switching being used.  
When this alarm threshold is exceeded, the stress on the switch must be checked and
if required, the switch should be replaced.

**Note:**
  
For p6711 = 0, the monitoring for the upper alarm threshold is switched out.

### r6713[0...5] Switch switching operations, total

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Displays the total number of switching operations of the switch.  
The total switching operations are determined as follows:  
r6713 = r6714 + r6715 * p6712  
r6713: Switching operations, total  
r6714: Switching operations in a no-current condition  
r6715: Switching operations under load  
p6712: Weighting factor for switching operations under load

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6710, p6711, p6712, p6714, p6715  
Refer to:
A07322, A07323

### p6714[0...5] Switch switching operations in a no-current condition

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 100000 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Displays and sets the number of switching operations of the switch under no-current
conditions.  
When setting the parameter, the value is accepted without confirmation.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6713, p6715  
Refer to:
A07322, A07323

**Note:**
  
The total number of switching operations of the switch under no-current conditions
is saved in a non-volatile fashion on all of the Power Stack Adapters (PSA) being
used.  
Inconsistency is not monitored and there is no automatic alignment of the switching
operations for several Power Stack Adapters. The values of the first Power Stack Adapter
are read and evaluated.

[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6712, r6713, p6714  
Refer to:
A07322, A07323

**Note:**
  
The total number of switching operations of the switch under load is saved in a non-volatile
fashion on all of the Power Stack Adapters (PSA) being used.  
Inconsistency is not monitored and there is no automatic alignment of the switching
operations for several Power Stack Adapters. The values of the first Power Stack Adapter
are read and evaluated.

### p6740[0...n] PSA DC link voltage actual value positive VdcP fine calibration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 200.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the fine calibration for the positive DC link voltage actual value for the Power
Stack Adapter (PSA).  
The measured actual value is multiplied by this factor.

### r6760[0...n] CO: PSA actual value channel 0 value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the value of actual value channel 0 (X70) for the
Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
The value is displayed corresponding to the set display mode (p6770).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6770

**Note:**
  
The actual value is scaled to the maximum mapping range of the shunt (-100.00 ...
100.00).  
Display value = 100.00 applies: The actual value sensing has failed.  
Display value = 9999.99 applies: The actual value sensing is not available.

### p6770 PSA actual value channels display mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 6 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the required display mode for actual value channels for the Power Stack Adapter
(PSA).  
The values for actual value channels 0 ... 9 are displayed in parameter r6760 ...
r6769.  
Depending on the display mode, in addition to the actual values, other information
is also displayed.  
For value = 2:  
- if necessary without offset and with fine calibration.  
If value = 3:  
- see for parameter p6950.  
If value = 5:  
- 0 = inactive, 1 = VdcP, 2 = VdcN, 4 = iU, -4 = -iU, 5 = iV, -5 = -iV, 6 = iW, -6
= -iW, 7 = uUV, -7 = uVU, 8 = uWV, -8 = uVW, 9 = uUW, -9 = uWU  
If value = 6:  
- only for service purposes.

**Value:**
  
0:
Raw actual value (with offset)  
1:
Actual value used in the closed-loop control  
2:
Offset value calculated  
3:
Threshold value for monitoring  
4:
Max. resolution range  
5:
Channel assignment  
6:
Offset adjustment status

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6760, r6761, r6762, r6763, r6764, r6765, r6766, r6767, r6768, r6769, p6902, p6903,
p6904

**Note:**
  
For p6770 = 1 the following applies:  
- p6902, p6903 and p6904 define whether the offset calculation should be taken into
account.  
For p6770 = 6 the following applies:  
- 16 ... 31 = "Take into account offset"  
- 32 ... 47 = "Offset calibration active"  
- 48 ... 63 = "Take into account offset and offset calibration active "  
- 64 ... 127 = "Offset calculation inhibited"

### p6789 Actual value sensing synclost suppression time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [µs] | 163 [µs] | [ 0 ] 10 [µs] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the synclost suppression time for the actual values sensing.

### p6790[0...1] Main fan nominal differential pressure threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9805 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Fan), VECTOR3P (Fan), A_INFMV (Fan), B_INFMV (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [Pa] | 1000.00 [Pa] | [ 0 ] 600.00 [Pa] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the differential pressure for the alarm threshold for the main fan.  
This value is an absolute value with units of Pascal (Pa). The other parameter values
refer to this.  
Alarm A49111 is output when this differential pressure is fallen below.  
When the differential pressure is fallen below, the redundant main fan is simultaneously
switched on (if one exists) and Alarm A49118 is output.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Differential pressure actual value 1  
[
1]:
Differential pressure actual value 2 (optional)

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6791  
Refer to:
A49111

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the differential pressure for the fault threshold for the main fan.  
An appropriate fault is output when this differential pressure is undershot.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:

1]:

### p6792[0...3] Main fan minimum differential pressure change when switching

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9805 |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.10 [%] | 100.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 20.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the minimum differential pressure change for the alarm threshold when switching
on/switching off the fans.  
If at least this differential pressure is not established within the monitoring time
when switching on/switching off, then an appropriate alarm is output.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Main fan differential pressure actual value 1  
[
1]:
Redundant main fan diff. pressure actual value 1 (optional)  
[
2]:
Main fan differential pressure actual value 2 (optional)  
[
3]:
Redundant main fan diff. pressure actual value 2 (optional)

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6790, p6794  
Refer to:
A49114, A49115

### p6793[0...1] Main fan differential press. change when filter mats are clogged

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Fan), VECTOR3P (Fan), VECTORSL (Fan), A_INFMV (Fan), B_INFMV (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.10 [%] | 100.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 2.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the minimum differential pressure change when the filter mats are clogged.  
If the differential pressure, during operation, enters a band above the alarm threshold
(p6790 + p6793) without such a differential pressure change occurring, then an appropriate
alarm is output as the filter mats are clogged.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Differential pressure actual value 1  
[
1]:
Differential pressure actual value 2 (optional)

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6790, p6796  
Refer to:
A49117

### p6796[0...1] Main fan, differential pressure tolerance

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9805 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Fan), VECTOR3P (Fan), VECTORSL (Fan), A_INFMV (Fan), B_INFMV (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 20.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 5.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the tolerance for the differential pressure.  
This tolerance value is used in the following cases:  
- hysteresis for the prewarning threshold for clogged filter mats (p6790 + 2 x p6796),
A49117.  
- hysteresis for the alarm threshold of the main fan and the switch-on threshold of
the redundant main fan (p6790 + p6796), A49111, A49118.  
- plausibility monitoring differential pressure actual value (zero - p6796, hysteresis
1 Pa), F49112.  
- plausibility monitoring differential pressure actual value (maximum pressure actual
value 1000 Pa - p6796, hysteresis p6796 x p6790), A49115.  
- hysteresis value for alarm A49111, A49118.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Differential pressure actual value 1  
[
1]:
Differential pressure actual value 2 (optional)

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6790  
Refer to:
A49111, F49112, A49115, A49117, A49118

### p6811 Braking chopper braking resistor

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9841 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Brk Mod ext mot, Tech_ctrl), VECTOR3P (Brk Mod ext mot, Tech_ctrl), A_INFMV (Brk Mod ext) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [ohm] | 10000.00 [ohm] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [ohm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the ohmic value for the braking resistor of the braking chopper (value at 20°C).  
The value of the resistors connected to the individual braking choppers should be
entered here.  
The resistance value between the positive and negative rails of the DC link should
always be specified - also for a split resistor with connected center point.

**Note:**
  
For parallel circuit configurations, all of the braking resistors connected to a drive
must be identical.  
It is not permissible that commissioning (p0010 = 0) is completed nor can it be completed
without having entered a value &gt; 0.

### p6812 Braking chopper switching frequency

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the switching frequency for the braking chopper.  
The factory setting depends on the power unit being used.

**Notice:**
  
If the switching frequency is increased, this reduces the permissible load duty cycle.

### r6822.0...3 CO/BO: Braking chopper status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9841 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Brk Mod ext mot, Tech_ctrl), VECTOR3P (Brk Mod ext mot, Tech_ctrl), A_INFMV (Brk Mod ext) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the status word of the braking chopper.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Commissioning not completed | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Precontrol power negative (regenerative operation) | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Positive arm clocked | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Negative arm clocked | No | Yes | - |

### p6825[0...1] BI: Braking chopper braking resistor therm protection signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Brk Mod ext mot, Tech_ctrl), VECTOR3P (Brk Mod ext mot, Tech_ctrl), A_INFMV (Brk Mod ext), BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the protection of the braking resistor when an overtemperature
condition occurs.  
For a 1 signal, the corresponding alarm or fault is initiated.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Overtemperature alarm  
[
1]:
Overtemperature fault

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F06912, A06914

### p6830[0...n] PSA offset actual value channel 0

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -100000.00 [%] | 100000.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Saved offset value from the actual value channel 0 (X70) in the selected mode (p6902
= 2, p6903 = 2) for the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6902, p6903

**Note:**
  
100 % corresponds to the resolution range of the particular actual value channel (the
resolution range is displayed with setting p6770 = 4).  
Depending on the setting of p6902 or p6903, the offset of value is determined and
cannot be manually set.

### p6850[0...n] PSA analog output 0 AND logic operation value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9976 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0000 hex | FFFF FFFF hex | [ 0 ] 0000 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the value to be ANDed with the signal source in connector input p6840 for analog
output 0 (X332.15/X332.16) of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
Signal sources with data type Unsigned16/Unsigned32 are ANDed with this selected value.  
With the factory setting of p6860 and p6870 the result is displayed as follows at
the analog output:  
- Result of the AND logic operation = 1 --&gt; analog output = 10 V  
- Result of the AND logic operation = 0 --&gt; analog output = 5 V

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6840

### r6888[0...3] CO: PSA analog outputs calibration signals

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9976 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Calibration signals for the analog outputs of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
For the factory settings of p6860 to p6867 and p6870 to p6877, by appropriately interconnecting
connector input CI: p6840 to p6847 with r6888[0...3], the required calibration signals
are effective at the analog output.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Constant value -1.0 --&gt; 0 V  
[
1]:
Constant value 0.0 --&gt; 5 V  
[
2]:
Constant value +1.0 --&gt; 10 V  
[
3]:
Sawtooth with 1 Hz from -1.0 ... +1.0 --&gt; 0 ... 10 V

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6840

**Note:**
  
When setting the scaling factor (p6842) and offset (p6843) to the factory setting,
the following apply:  
CI: p6840 ... p6847 interconnected with r6888[0] --&gt; analog output = 0 V  
CI: p6840 ... p6847 interconnected with r6888[1] --&gt; analog output = 5 V  
CI: p6840 ... p6847 interconnected with r6888[2] --&gt; analog output = 10 V  
CI: p6840 ... p6847 interconnected with r6888[3] --&gt; analog output = sawtooth 0 ...
10 V with 1 Hz

### p6902 Current actual values, offset mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the offset mode for the current actual value sensing.  
If the mode is enabled (p6902 = 0), for an operating inhibit, the offset is determined
and when operation is enabled, the determined offset is taken into account for the
actual value calculation.  
For the inhibited mode (p6902 = 1), the offset is determined – but is not taken into
account for the actual value calculation.  
When the mode is enabled (p6902 = 2), for the operating inhibit, for 5 seconds, the
offset is determined as a fixed constant, and is automatically entered into the appropriate
parameter (p6830 ... p6839). It is not possible to manually enter the offset in p6830
... p6839

**Value:**
  
0:
Offset calculation enabled  
1:
Offset calculation inhibited  
2:
Fixed offset enabled

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6575

**Note:**
  
Offset mode can only be set for actual value sensing functions that are available
in the hardware.

**Description:**
  
Sets the offset mode for voltage actual value sensing.  
When the mode is enabled (p6903 = 0) the operating inhibit of the offset is determined.  
For the inhibited mode (p6903 = 1), the offset is determined – but is not taken into
account for the actual value calculation.  
When the mode is enabled (p6903 = 2), for the operating inhibit, for 5 seconds, the
offset is determined as a fixed constant, and is automatically entered into the appropriate
parameter (p6830 ... p6839). It is not possible to manually enter the offset in p6830
... p6839

**Value:**
  
0:
Offset calculation enabled  
1:
Offset calculation inhibited  
2:
Fixed offset enabled

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6575

**Note:**
  
Offset mode can only be set for actual value sensing functions that are available
in the hardware.

### r6916[0...n] PSA actual value channel communication error

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays communication errors of the actual value channels (data exchange between
AVT Combi and the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
Interpret hexadecimal:  
0x0001: actual value channel 1 (PSA.X70) sync-lost error.  
0x0002: actual value channel 2 (PSA.X71) sync-lost error.  
0x0004: actual value channel 3 (PSA.X72) sync-lost error.  
0x0008: actual value channel 4 (PSA.X73) sync-lost error.  
0x0010: actual value channel 5 (PSA.X74) sync-lost error.  
0x0020: actual value channel 6 (PSA.X75) sync-lost error.  
0x0040: actual value channel 7 (PSA.X76) sync-lost error.  
0x0080: actual value channel 8 (PSA.X77) sync-lost error.  
0x0100: actual value channel 9 (PSA.X78) sync-lost error.  
0x0200: actual value channel 10 (PSA.X79) sync lost error.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F30105

**Note:**
  
AVT-Combi: Actual Value Transmitter = analog-digital converter module with optical
interface

| 01 | Status V2 Phase U | OK | Fault | - |
| 02 | Status V3 Phase U | OK | Fault | - |
| 03 | Status V4 Phase U | OK | Fault | - |
| 04 | Status V1 Phase V | OK | Fault | - |
| 05 | Status V2 Phase V | OK | Fault | - |
| 06 | Status V3 Phase V | OK | Fault | - |
| 07 | Status V4 Phase V | OK | Fault | - |

| 09 | Status V2 Phase W | OK | Fault | - |
| 10 | Status V3 Phase W | OK | Fault | - |
| 11 | Status V4 Phase W | OK | Fault | - |

| 17 | Status V2 phase U valve group B | OK | Fault | - |
| 18 | Status V3 phase U valve group B | OK | Fault | - |
| 19 | Status V4 phase U valve group B | OK | Fault | - |
| 20 | Status V1 phase V valve group B | OK | Fault | - |
| 21 | Status V2 phase V valve group B | OK | Fault | - |

| 23 | Status V4 phase V valve group B | OK | Fault | - |

Sets the weighting of the linearization of the power unit.  
p6926 = 0 % --&gt; The linearization is disabled.

### p6938[0...8] Switching angle for constant modulation depth

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 16384 | [ 0 ] 7963   [ 1 ] 9043   [ 2 ] 10714   [ 3 ] 12712   [ 4 ] 13704   [ 5 ] 15869   [ 6 ] 0   [ 7 ] 0   [ 8 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the angle for the pulse pattern that can be freely defined for operation with
constant modulation depth.  
If line supply resonance effects are excited as a result of the default settings,
then the pulse pattern used as basis can be modified.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3400, p6935, p6936, p6937, p6939

**Caution:**
  
Only authorized Siemens service personnel may modify this parameter after prior discussion
with Customer Support.

**Notice:**
  
The number of angle entries different to zero must match the pulse number.

**Note:**
  
The angles should be entered in an increasing sequence.  
Zeros can be entered in decreasing order.

### p6940 Crowbar firing operating mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the operating mode to crowbar fire the power semiconductors for IGCT drive converters
(e.g. if an overvoltage occurs, in addition to the crowbar thyristor).  
For IGBT converters, this parameter is not taken into account. Further, the setting
of this parameter is only relevant if the drive converter is assigned a crowbar thyristor
(SM150: line-side converter; crowbar firing of the motor-side converter is realized
using a trip line).  
Crowbar firing is realized with a delay depending on the selected operating mode:  
p6940 = 0:  
Delay time relative to firing the crowbar thyristor is defined using p6941.  
p6940 = 1:  
Maximum delay time relative firing the crowbar thryristor is defined using p6941 if
the feedback signal "LSS_OFF" is available, then crowbar firing is realized correspondingly
earlier.  
p6940 = 2:  
Crowbar firing inhibited.

**Value:**
  
0:
Crowbar firing enable  
1:
Crowbar firing with the LSS open  
2:
Crowbar firing inhibited

**Note:**
  
LSS: Line Side Switch

**Description:**
  
Sets the type of analog inputs on the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
In addition, the associated switches S5 and S6 must be appropriately set.  
AI 0: S5.0 = V --&gt; voltage input, S5.0 = I --&gt; current input  
AI 1: S5.1 = V --&gt; voltage input, S5.1 = I --&gt; current input  
AI 2: S6.0 = V --&gt; voltage input, S6.0 = I --&gt; current input  
AI 3: S6.1 = V --&gt; voltage input, S6.1 = I --&gt; current input  
The scaling (final value) can be set using parameters p17460, p17463, p17466, p17469.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | PSA AI 0 , type | Unipolar 10V | Unipolar 20mA | - |
| 01 | PSA AI 0, zero point offset | No | 2V / 4mA | - |
| 02 | PSA AI 0, check plausibility | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | PSA AI 1, type | Unipolar 10V | Unipolar 20mA | - |
| 05 | PSA AI 1, zero point offset | No | 2V / 4mA | - |
| 06 | PSA AI 1, check plausibility | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | PSA AI 2, type | Unipolar 10V | Unipolar 20mA | - |
| 09 | PSA AI 2, zero point offset | No | 2V / 4mA | - |
| 10 | PSA AI 2, check plausibility | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | PSA AI 3, type | Unipolar 10V | Unipolar 20mA | - |
| 13 | PSA AI 3, zero point offset | No | 2V / 4mA | - |
| 14 | PSA AI 3, check plausibility | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6946, r6947, r6948, r6949, p17460, r17462, p17463, r17465, p17466, r17468, p17469,
r17471  
Refer to:
A30125

**Notice:**
  
For operation as a voltage input/current input, switch S5.0, S5.1, S6.0 or S6.1 must
be appropriately set.

**Note:**
  
The settings of the individual inputs can only be changed if these are not preset
by the hardware description (DESC0000).

### p6945[0...n] PSA temperature sensor type

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9985 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Terminals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Sets the sensor type for Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
Temperature input (Pt100) is enabled. The input is monitored for wire breakage and
short-circuit.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | PSA AT 0 , type | Open | PT100 | - |
| 04 | PSA AT 1, type | Open | PT100 | - |
| 08 | PSA AT 2, type | Open | PT100 | - |
| 12 | PSA AT 3, type | Open | PT100 | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6951, r6952, r6953, r6954

**Note:**
  
The settings of the individual inputs can only be changed if these are not preset
by the hardware description (DESC0000).

### p6950[0...2] Actual value monitoring threshold value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 110.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 5.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the threshold value for the actual value monitoring function.  
The value refers to the maximum resolution range of the particular actual value channel.  
For p6770 = 3, this value is displayed for the actual value channel involved in the
corresponding parameter (r6760 and following).  
The total threshold value is displayed there for p6770 = 4.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
DC link voltage  
[
1]:
Output current  
[
2]:
Output voltage

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F30106

### r6951[0...n] CO: PSA temperature input 0 value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9985 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Terminals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**21_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2006 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [°C] | - [°C] | [ ] - [°C] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output of the smoothed value at temperature input 0 (TEMP 0)
on the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6955, p6959  
Refer to:
A30018, A30019

**Note:**
  
The input signal can be smoothed for all PSA using p6959[4].  
For 4-conductor temperature sensors, the following applies  
I_IN 0: X333.13  
TEMP 0-: X333.14  
TEMP 0+: X333.15  
I_OUT 0: X333.16  
For 2-conductor temperature sensors, the following external jumpers are required:  
X333.13 - X333.14  
X333.15 - X333.16  
For a display value = -200 °C, the following applies:  
The input is not enabled via parameter p6945 or via the hardware description data.  
For a display value = -199 °C, the following applies:  
An implausible measured value is available at the input.

### p6955[0...n] PSA temperature input 0 offset

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -100.0 [°C] | 100.0 [°C] | [ 0 ] 0.0 [°C] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the temperature offset for the PT100 measured value at PSA temperature input
0 (PSA TEMP 0).  
If there is a difference between the temperature displayed in r6951 and the actual
temperature, then this offset can be entered in this parameter. thereby compensating
for the difference.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0037, r6951, p6959

### p6959[0...7] PSA analog inputs smoothing time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Terminals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [ms] | 1000.0 [ms] | [ 0 ] 0.0 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the smoothing time constant of the 1st order low-pass filter for the analog inputs
on the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
This setting is the same for all PDS.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Analog input 0  
[
1]:
Analog input 1  
[
2]:
Analog input 2  
[
3]:
Analog input 3  
[
4]:
Temperature input 0  
[
5]:
Temperature input 1  
[
6]:
Temperature input 2  
[
7]:
Temperature input 3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0037, r6951, r6952, r6954, p6955

**Note:**
  
AI: Analog Input  
TEMP: temperature sensor input PT100

### p6960 U/f table number

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6760 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 31 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the requested number to select the U/f table.  
This parameter is automatically pre-set according to the converter type and application.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6972, r6974  
Refer to:
F30910

**Warning:**
  
Changing this pre-set value can have very serious consequences (e.g. instability,
overload, noisy operation) and therefore should only be changed by an expert.

### p6975 Switching frequency reduction number of reduction stages

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6760 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 5 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the number of stages for the switching frequency reduction.  
The switching frequency is linearly reduced and in stages for output frequency below
the threshold parameterized in p6976.  
Example:  
For a 5-stage frequency reduction, the reduction starts at 50% and is reduced in stages
with increasing output frequency. When the output frequency threshold is reached,
then there is no longer any reduction.

**Value:**
  
0:
None frequency reduction  
1:
Frequency reduction 1-stage  
2:
Frequency reduction 2-stage  
3:
Frequency reduction 3-stage  
4:
Frequency reduction 4-stage  
5:
Frequency reduction 5-stage

**Notice:**
  
When the switching frequency reduction is activated, then carefully note the effects
of the lower switching frequency on the drive (increased current harmonics, mechanical
excitation, nose, etc.).

**Note:**
  
p6975 = 0: No switching frequency reduction  
p6975 = 1: Switching frequency reduction 1-stage, starting at 10 %  
...  
p6975 = 5: Switching frequency reduction 5-stage, starting at 50 %  
Independent of the setting of this parameter, the switching frequency is multiplied
by the factor parameterized in p6961.

### p6976 Switching frequency reduction output frequency threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6760 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [Hz] | 340.28235E36 [Hz] | [ 0 ] 5.00 [Hz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the frequency threshold for the switching frequency reduction.  
For p6975 &gt; 0, output frequencies below this threshold are reduced.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6960, r6965  
Refer to:
F07085

**Note:**
  
The lower the selected switching frequency (using p6960), the lower the permissible
output frequency threshold.  
When exiting the commissioning (p0010 = 0), the value for the threshold is subject
to a plausibility check and if required, internally reduced to a permissible value.
In this case, an appropriate message is output.

### r6992.0...15 CO/BO: Recorder status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144, 8145 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the status word of the recorder.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Internal activation | Not set | Set | - |
| 01 | External activation | Not set | Set | - |
| 02 | Internal trigger | Not set | Set | - |
| 03 | External trigger 1.1 | Not set | Set | - |
| 04 | External trigger 1.2 | Not set | Set | - |
| 05 | External trigger 1.3 | Not set | Set | - |
| 06 | External trigger 1.4 | Not set | Set | - |
| 07 | External trigger 2.1 | Not set | Set | - |
| 08 | External trigger 2.2 | Not set | Set | - |
| 09 | External trigger 2.3 | Not set | Set | - |
| 10 | Hardware trigger | Not set | Set | - |
| 11 | Data buffering running | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Post trigger time running | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Data being stored | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Data buffer full | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Trigger group signal | Not set | Set | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6993, p6994, r6997, p6998, p6999  
Refer to:
A49998

### p6993[0...5] Recorder trigger 2 bit mask

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0000 hex | FFFF FFFF hex | [ 0 ] 0001 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the bit mask for trigger signal 2 (p6994) of the recorder.  
External trigger 2.1 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[0] and the bit mask in p6993[0].  
External trigger 2.2 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[1] and the bit mask in p6993[1].  
External trigger 2.3 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[2] and the bit mask in p6993[2].  
External trigger 2.4 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[3] and the bit mask in p6993[3].  
External trigger 2.5 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[4] and the bit mask in p6993[4].  
External trigger 2.6 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[5] and the bit mask in p6993[5].

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
External trigger 2.1  
[
1]:
External trigger 2.2  
[
2]:
External trigger 2.3  
[
3]:
External trigger 2.4  
[
4]:
External trigger 2.5  
[
5]:
External trigger 2.6

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6994

**Note:**
  
If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.
This means that any parameter assignments that were made could be overwritten.

## SINAMICS Parameter ACTIVE INFEED CONTROLMV 06994 - 08893

SINAMICS Parameter ACTIVE INFEED CONTROLMV 06994 - 08893

### p6994[0...5] CI: Recorder trigger 2 signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Integer32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for trigger 2 of the recorder.  
External trigger 2.1 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[0] and the bit mask in p6993[0].  
External trigger 2.2 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[1] and the bit mask in p6993[1].  
External trigger 2.3 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[2] and the bit mask in p6993[2].  
External trigger 2.4 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[3] and the bit mask in p6993[3].  
External trigger 2.5 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[4] and the bit mask in p6993[4].  
External trigger 2.6 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[5] and the bit mask in p6993[5].

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
External trigger 2.1  
[
1]:
External trigger 2.2  
[
2]:
External trigger 2.3  
[
3]:
External trigger 2.4  
[
4]:
External trigger 2.5  
[
5]:
External trigger 2.6

p6993

If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal sources to activate and trigger the recorder.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
External activation  
[
1]:
External trigger 1.1  
[
2]:
External trigger 1.2  
[
3]:
External trigger 1.3  
[
4]:
External trigger 1.4

**Note:**
  
If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.
This means that any parameter assignments that were made could be overwritten.  
For index [0]:  
The external recorder activation extends the internal activation. If the recorder
has already been activated internally, then it can be externally triggered via p6998[1..4]
or p6994[0..2], without requiring that p6998[0] = 1.

### p6999[0...8] Recorder parameterization

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144, 8145 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2000 | [ 0 ] 1   [ 1 ] 2000   [ 2 ] 1900   [ 3 ] 0   [ 4 ] 0   [ 5 ] 100   [ 6 ] 1   [ 7 ] 1   [ 8 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Setting to parameterize the recorder.  
The recorder supplies up to 256 internal variables (depending on the parameterization).
The maximum recording time is 2000 ms. The variables are acquired in an integer multiple
of the current controller sampling time – and a pretrigger can be set. The values
can then be acyclically written to the memory card.  
The development and system test departments have the software necessary to decode
the content.  
Normally, this recorder is always enabled. However, if problems occur for topologies
with a low remaining CPU time, then it can be deactivated using this parameter (this
saves approx. 5 ... 20 µs CPU time).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Enable  
[
1]:
Recording time  
[
2]:
Pretrigger time internal trigger  
[
3]:
Output message  
[
4]:
Recording factor  
[
5]:
Pretrigger time external trigger  
[
6]:
Hardware trace in synchronism  
[
7]:
Hardware trigger active  
[
8]:
Recording sampling time factor

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A49998

**Note:**
  
If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.
This means that any parameter assignments that were made could be overwritten.  
For index [0]:  
Enables or disables the function.  
p6999[0] = 0  
Inhibits the function.  
p6999[0] = 1  
Enables the function. A POWER ON is required after the enable.  
p6999[0] = 20  
Sets the factory setting for p6999, p6996, p6993, p6994, p6998.  
For index [1]:  
Sets the recording time, [ms]. The value is discretized with the current controller
sampling time.  
For index [2]:  
Sets the pretrigger time [ms] of the internal trigger. This time is included in the
recording time and cannot be longer than the recording time p6999[1]. The value is
discretized with the current controller sampling time.  
For index [3]:  
Enables or inhibits the output of message A49998 when a trigger event is initiated.  
For index [4]:  
p6999[4] = n, n = 0 ... 4  
Recording with the factor, which extends the record time p6999[1] and the pre-trigger
time p6999[2] 2^n times, and reduces the number of signals 2^n times.  
Example:  
p6999[1] = 2000, p6999[2] = 1000, p6999[4] = 4  
--&gt; recording time: 2000 ms * 2^4 = 32 s, pre-trigger time:1000 ms * 2^4 = 16 s, number
of signals 128/16 = 8  
When setting p6999[4] to 0, the results are as follows:  
--&gt; recording time: 2000 ms, pre-trigger time: 1000 ms, number of signals: 128  
The maximum number of signals depends on the drive object.  
For index [5]:  
Sets the pretrigger time [ms] of the external trigger. This time is included in the
recording time and cannot be longer than the recording time p6999[1]. The value is
discretized with the current controller sampling time.  
For index [6]:  
Activating/deactivating wait time for a hardware trace to be completely uploaded.  
p6999[6] = 1  
After a trigger event, the system waits for the hardware trace upload to be completed.
A new trigger is not possible until this upload has been completed.  
p6999[6] = 0  
After a trigger event, the system does NOT wait for the hardware trace upload to be
completed. Recorder trace can be immediately re-triggered.  
For index [7]:  
Deactivates/activates the hardware trigger. When p6999[7] = 0, the hardware trigger
is deactivated, and a hardware fault means that the recorder is no longer triggered.  
For index [8]:  
Sets the factor for the recording sampling time.  
Example:  
p6999[8] = 2 --&gt; sampling time to record/trace signals is 2 x current controller sampling
time  
After a power on, this parameter has the same value as before the change.

### r7001.0...2 CO/BO: Par_connection power units active/inactiv status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Modulation | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the state "Active/Inactiv" of the power unit.  
Power units are selected and deselected using p7006.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Subsystem 1 active | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Subsystem 2 active | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Subsystem 3 active | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p7006

### r7002.0...2 CO/BO: PU_redundancy fault display

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output to display subsystem faults.  
Messages F05080, F05081 and N05082 are output for a subsystem in a parallel connection
that is not ready for operation. If the subsystem that is not ready for operation
is deactivated, then in the case of function "Redundant operation", operation of other
active and fault-free power units can still continue although there is a subsystem
fault.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Subsystem 1: Fault present | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Subsystem 2: Fault present | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Subsystem 3: Fault present | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r7001, r7100, r7101, r7102, r7103, r7104, r7105  
Refer to:
F05080, F05081, N05082

**Notice:**
  
Faults that have occurred are only saved in the ring buffer (r7100[0...99] ... r7105[0...99]),
and for a deactivated subsystem or a subsystem in redundant operation, are not signaled
as is generally the case.  
Faults in subsystems 1, 2 or 3 are signaled using F05080, F05081 or N05082.

**Note:**
  
The fault state of a subsystem is only derived from the faults that are assigned to
the subsystem.  
Other faults are not taken into account here.

### p7006[0...n] BI: Activate/deactivate power unit, signal source

|  |  |  |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source to activate/deactivate a power unit.  
BI: p7006 = 1 signal:  
The relevant power unit is selected.  
BI: p7006 = 0 signal:  
The relevant power unit is deselected.

**Dependency:**

r7001

**Notice:**
  
The parameter may be protected as a result of p0922 or p2079 and cannot be changed.

**Note:**
  
Status "Active/inactive" of the power unit is indicated in r7001.

NO ALARM  
0:
ALARM  
1:
OFF1  
2:
OFF2  
4:
OFF3  
16:
DCBRAKE  
32:
STOP1  
64:
STOP2  
256:
ENCODER  
1024:
NONE

**Description:**
  
Fault buffer for faults in power units connected in parallel.  
Displays the fault code.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r7109

**Note:**
  
The last fault that occurred is documented in index 0.  
The parameter is reset to 0 at POWER ON.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Displays the relative system runtime in days when the fault/alarm occurred.  
Ring buffer for faults and alarms that have occurred for power units connected in
parallel.

**Notice:**
  
The time comprises r7123 (days) and r7102 (milliseconds).  
The time display depends on the selected mode (p3100).

**Note:**
  
The last fault case that occurred is documented in index 0.  
The parameter is reset to 0 at POWER ON.

### r7760 Write protection/know-how protection status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TECBASE, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, TB30, BMM2C, ENC, HUB | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Displays the status for the write protection and know-how protection.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Write protection active | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Know-how protection active | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Know-how protection temporarily withdrawn | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Know-how protection cannot be deactivated | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Extended copy protection is active | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Basic copy protection is active | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Trace and measuring functions for diagnostic purposes active | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Reserved, Siemens-internal | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p7761, p7765

**Note:**
  
KHP: Know-How Protection  
For bit 00:  
Write protection can be activated/deactivated via p7761 on the Control Unit.  
For bit 01:  
The know-how protection can be activated by entering a password (p7766 ... p7768).  
For bit 02:  
If it has already been activated, know-how protection can be temporarily deactivated
by entering the valid password in p7766. In this case, bit 1 = 0 and bit 2 = 1 offset.  
For bit 03:  
Know-how protection cannot be deactivated, as p7766 is not entered in the OEM exception
list (only the factory setting is possible). This bit is only set if know-how protection
is active (bit 1 = 1) and p7766 has not been entered in the OEM exception list.  
For bit 04:  
When know-how protection has been activated, the contents of the memory card (parameter
and DCC data) can be additionally protected against being used with other memory cards/Control
Units. This bit is only set if know-how protection is active and in p7765.0 is set
= 1.  
For bit 05:  
When know-how protection has been activated, the contents of the memory card (parameter
and DCC data) can be additionally protected against being used with other memory cards.
This bit is only set if know-how protection is active and p7765.1 is set = 1 and p7765.0
is set = 0.  
For bit 06:  
When know-how protection is activated, the drive data can be traced using the device
trace function. This bit is only set if know-how protection is active and p7765.2
is set = 1.  
For bit 12:  
Together with p7755, the bit is used to monitor write protection.  
Bit = 1, if p7755 is not equal to 0 and write protection is active (r7760.0 = 1).  
Bit = 0, if write protection was deactivated. p7755 is set to 0, and when write protection
is activated again, bit 12 remains at 0.

to.

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |

to.

### p7770 NVRAM action

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, TB30, ENC, HUB | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the action to be executed for NVRAM data.  
At the end of the action the value is automatically set to 0.

**Value:**
  
0:
Inactive  
1:
Load NVRAM data to parameters  
2:
Load parameters to NVRAM  
3:
Reset

**Notice:**
  
After action p7770 = 1 no more pulses may be enabled.  
After action p7770 = 2, it is essential that parameters are backed up (p0977 = 1)
and that a warm restart is then performed (p0009 = 30, p0976 = 2, 3). This will apply
the values written.

**Note:**
  
For value = 1:  
This action loads the NVRAM data to the parameters.  
For value = 2:  
This action loads the parameters to the NVRAM.  
If value = 3:  
This action sets parameters p7771 ... p7774 to the factory setting.  
It is recommended to avoid placing unnecessary load on the subsequent upload/download
operation.

|  |  |  |

2.6

Sets the mode for the sub-boot.

**Value:**
  
0:
Sub-boot manual  
1:
Sub-boot automatic

**Note:**
  
For p7857 = 0 (manual sub-boot) the following applies:  
The parameter should be set to 1 to start the sub-boot.

### r7871[0...15] Configuration changes drive object

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the configuration changes on the drive object.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Sum of the following indices  
[
1]:
p0010, p0107, p0108  
[
2]:
Drive object name (p0199)  
[
3]:
Structure-relevant parameters (e.g. p0180)  
[
4]:
BICO interconnections  
[
5]:
Activate/deactivate drive object  
[
6]:
Data backup required  
[
7]:
Activate/deactivate component  
[
8]:
Reference or changeover parameters (e.g. p2000)  
[
9]:
Parameter count through Drive Control Chart (DCC)  
[
10]:
p0107, p0108  
[
11]:
Reserved  
[
12]:
Write protection and know-how protection status  
[
13]:
Reserved  
[
14]:
Reserved  
[
15]:
Reserved

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r7868, r7870

**Note:**
  
For index [0]:  
When changing one of the following indices, then the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [1]:  
Drive object commissioning: When changing p0010, p0107, p0108, p0171, p0172 or p0173,
the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [2]:  
Drive object name. When changing p0199, the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [3]:  
Drive object structure. When changing a parameter that is relevant for the structure
(e.g. number of data sets), the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [4]:  
Drive object BICO interconnections. When changing r3977, the value in this index is
incremented.  
For index [5]:  
Drive object activity: When changing p0105, the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [6]:  
Drive object, data save.  
0: There are no parameter changes to save.  
1: There are parameter changes to save.  
For index [7]:  
Drive object component activity: When changing either p0125 or p0145, the value in
this index is incremented.  
For index [8]:  
Drive object changeover of units. When changing reference or changeover parameters
(e.g. p2000, p0304), the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [9]:  
Drive object parameter count. When changing the number of parameters by loading Drive
Control Chart (DCC), the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [10]:  
Drive object configuration. When changing p0107, p0108, p0171, p0172 or p0173, the
value in this index is incremented.

### p8612[0...1] CAN drive object server SDO

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN (CAN), CU_MV_DP (CAN), VECTORMV (CAN), VECTORGL (CAN), VECTOR3P (CAN), VECTORSL (CAN), VECTORM2C (CAN), VECTORDM (CAN), A_INFMV (CAN), B_INFMV (CAN), A_INFM2C (CAN), BMM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0581 hex | 8000 067F hex | [ 0 ] 8000 0000 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the identifier (client/server and server/client) of the additional SDO server.  
Using these SDO servers, it is possible to access manufacturer-specific CANopen objects
of the supported drive objects.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
COB-ID drive object from the client to the server  
[
1]:
COB-ID drive object from the server to the client

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r8610

**Note:**
  
SDO: Service Data Object  
Regarding the drive object Control Unit:  
- Corresponds to the CANopen object 1201 hex  
Regarding the drive object with closed-loop control functions:  
- Corresponds to the CANopen object 1202 hex + 1 * x (x: Drive number 0 ... 7)

### p8641 CAN Abort Connection Option Code

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 3 |

4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the fault response for a CAN communication error with output of fault F08700.  
Corresponds to the CANopen object 6007 hex.

**Value:**
  
0:
No response  
1:
OFF1  
2:
OFF2  
3:
OFF3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p2100, p2101  
Refer to:
F08700

**Note:**
  
When the factory setting is changed, the fault response of fault F08700 is written
to p2100 and p2101. In this particular case it is not possible to change the message
type using p2118 and p2119.

### p8700[0...1] CAN Receive PDO 1

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( 3) T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9204, 9206 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (CAN), VECTORGL (CAN), VECTOR3P (CAN), VECTORSL (CAN), VECTORM2C (CAN), VECTORDM (CAN), A_INFMV (CAN), B_INFMV (CAN), A_INFM2C (CAN), BMM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |

1).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
PDO COB-ID  
[
1]:
PDO transmission type

**Dependency:**
  
A valid COB-ID can only be set for the available (existing) channel.

**Note:**
  
Corresponds to the CANopen object 1400 hex + 40 hex * x (x: Drive number 0 ... 7).  
Transmission types 0, 1, FE and FF can be set.  
PDO: Process Data Object
